essence: Installs, tests, and maintains smoke‑ventilation systems that keep escape routes clear during a fire.

identity.title: Smoke Ventilation Technician
identity.summary: Installs and services smoke‑control systems, including automatic vents, fans, control panels, and extraction pathways to ensure safe evacuation during fires.

salary.notes: Technicians specialising in large commercial, industrial, or high‑rise smoke‑control systems can exceed £50k-£60k+.

qualifications: Training in smoke‑control and ventilation systems
Knowledge of fire‑safety regulations and BS EN 12101
Ability to test, fault‑find, and certify smoke‑vent systems
Understanding of electrical systems, fans, vents, and control panels

lifestyle.description: Smoke ventilation technicians work across high‑rise buildings, commercial sites, and industrial facilities installing and maintaining smoke‑control systems. The role involves electrical work, mechanical systems, diagnostics, and ensuring compliance with strict fire‑safety standards. It suits individuals who enjoy technical problem‑solving and contributing to life‑safety infrastructure.

habits: Tests vents, fans, and control systems regularly
Follows strict installation and compliance standards
Keeps accurate maintenance and certification records
Communicates clearly with building managers and contractors
Diagnoses faults in electrical and mechanical components
